utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 of these items appear. Each needs to be fully specified with all details as required: health_coverage (includes physical and mental health and costs – fully specified), property_coverage (home/contents insurance with costs – partially specified, only says 'monthly_cost_included_in_bundle' without explicit cost details, but still indicates cost inclusion), travel_coverage (travel insurance with costs – same as property_coverage, cost inclusion but not numeric), additional_services (enhancement services with costs – extra services listed but no costs), total_budget (complete monthly/annual cost breakdown – fully detailed). Therefore, fully specified with all required details (including explicit costs) are health_coverage and total_budget. Property_coverage, travel_coverage, and additional_services lack complete cost details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes: (1) a health_coverage section listing the 'LifeFirst Standard Plan'—this corresponds to entries under both 'physical_health' and 'mental_health' within 'primary_health_coverage', where 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' has a rating of 8 in both cases. (2) A property_coverage section that aligns with 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under 'property_protection.home_content', which has a rating of 8. (3) A travel_coverage section that matches 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under 'mobility_coverage.travel_insurance' with a rating of 7. (4) Additional services include 'telemedicine' and 'wellness programs', which map to 'Telemedicine Services' (rating 9) and 'Wellness Programs' (rating 9) under 'health_enhancement_services'. Thus, the AI assistant selected mid-tier (standard) plans instead of the top-rated premium ones.
